title: Lampranthus altistylus
taxonomy: Lampranthus altistylus is classified within the plant kingdom, specifically as a member of the family Aizoaceae. This family is a part of the larger order Caryophyllales. At the kingdom level, it is classified as Plantae, with its domain being Eukarya. It belongs to the division Magnoliophyta, which is further categorized under the phylum Angiospermophyta. This classification is based on various botanical and taxonomic studies.

intro: Lampranthus altistylus, commonly known as the pride of Table Mountain, is a succulent plant species native to South Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including the reduction of inflammation and the potential treatment of various conditions such as hypertension and diabetes due to its antioxidant and anti-diabetic properties. Bioactive constituents of Lampranthus altistylus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Lampranthus altistylus include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to leverage the plant's medicinal properties in traditional medicine practices.
